title: Report of a tax-farmer to the strategos, Aurelios Anoubion
summary: A monthly return for Pauni of receipts of taxation, written by the farmer of a tax upon sellers of two different kinds of pulse.

title: Notice of death
summary: Notice of the death of two relatives, sent to the scribes of the city by a hierotekton of Thoeris, Isis, Sarapis, and the associated gods, to Apolllonios and Didymos.

title: Bible. O.T. Apocrypha. Ecclesiasticus I, 1-9
summary: The first nine verses of Ecclesiasticus, or, The wisdom of Sirach (Jesus ben Sira). This is the first papyrus of this text. Verse 7, usually viewed as a doublet of verse 6, is omitted as in the chief uncial mss.
genre: Literary papyri, Identified authors; Biblical texts

title: Bible. N.T. John VI, 8-12, 17-22
summary: An eclectic text of portions of the sixth chapter of the Gospel of John, with differing order of words at verses 17-18, where many mss. differ.
date_text: Probably early or middle part of the 4th century A.D.
genre: Literary papyri, Identified authors; Biblical texts

title: Agathos' letter : to Aphrodite
summary: Letter accompanying a sealed order to be handed to a third person, asking for an immediate acknowledgement.

title: Lease of Land
summary: Beginning of a lease of 10 arourae of Idiotike ge at Talao from a senator of Antinoou polis to three brothers, half to be sown with wheat.

title: Weeaver's account of work.
summary: A weaver's account of work between the Egyptian months Mesore 3 and Thoth 6, with accounts in drachmae and occasional entries concerning rent of his loom.

title: Didyme's letter : to Atienateia.
summary: Didyme and her "sisters in the Lord" urge Atienateia, who still had a balance of 1,300 denarii to her credit, to indicate any further wants. Didyme refers to herself and "her sisters in the Lord," a formula for indicating that the writer is a Christian.
